text.title: AT Testing Strategy and Test Coverage
text.summary: Three-tier AT testing strategy, screen-reader and browser matrix, keyboard test key map, and common failure taxonomy.

rule.statement: Run automated scans in CI on every PR, manual SR/keyboard sweeps on every release, and user-with-disability sessions quarterly. Never substitute any tier for another.
rule.rationale: Automated tools catch 30–50% of real issues. The remaining 50–70% — dynamic content failures, focus-order errors, ARIA misuse on custom components — requires human screen-reader testing. Quarterly user sessions catch cognitive accessibility issues invisible to all automated and manual checks.

section.title: Screen Reader and Browser Matrix
section.p: Maintain a support matrix of three SR+browser pairs and own them completely. Recommended pairs: NVDA + Firefox (Windows, free), JAWS + Chrome (Windows, enterprise benchmark), VoiceOver + Safari (macOS/iOS, required for Apple platform claims). Also test: TalkBack + Chrome (Android, mobile SR); requires real device — simulator parity is poor.

section.title: Keyboard Test Keys
section.p: Required keyboard actions to verify in every interactive component test:

list.item: Tab / Shift+Tab — navigate forward and backward
Arrow keys — navigate within composite widgets (menus, tabs, trees, grids)
Escape — dismiss dialogs, close menus, exit modes
Skip link — navigate to main content without Tab traversal

section.title: Common Failure Taxonomy
section.p: Most frequent issues found in manual AT testing (in order of prevalence):

list.item: Missing form labels — input has no associated label element or aria-label
No skip navigation — keyboard users must Tab through full nav on every page
Inaccessible custom components — combobox, modal, tree, datagrid with incorrect ARIA roles or key behavior
Missing alt text — images convey content but have no text alternative
Poor focus management — modal opens without focus move; close returns focus to wrong element
Keyboard traps — Tab does not exit a component; Escape does not dismiss

rule.statement: Every AT bug report must include: WCAG 2.2 success criterion reference, minimal HTML repro snippet, suggested ARIA APG pattern with URL, and scope estimate (single component | layout-wide | system).
rule.rationale: AT bugs filed without WCAG citations and repros get postponed indefinitely. A scope estimate triggers the correct remediation path — a single-component fix vs. a design-system-level change have different owners and timelines.
